a, an indefinite article.definition: A small word. Use it before one thing. 'A' goes before consonant sounds. 'An' goes before vowel sounds.
a, an indefinite article.exampleSentence: I have a cat at home.
a, an indefinite article.contextPassage: Use 'a' or 'an' before new things. Say 'an apple' and 'a ball'.
distractors: A word that shows the thing is known. Use it before things you already talked about.
A word that shows more than one thing. Add it before big groups.
A word that asks a question about a thing. Use it to ask what something is.

about.definition: Moving here and there. Not staying in one place.
about.exampleSentence: The children are about in the garden.
about.contextPassage: When people are about, they are up and moving. They are not sitting or sleeping.
distractors: Staying very still in one place. Not moving at all.
Sleeping in a bed. Resting and not awake.
Walking very fast from one place to another place.

above.definition: The place where good people go after life. It is very high up in the sky.
above.exampleSentence: Grandma talks about above with a smile.
above.contextPassage: Some people believe above is a happy place. It is up high, far from here.
distractors: A place under the ground. It is very dark and deep.
A place far away on Earth. You can travel there by plane.
A place inside a big building. Many people go there to pray.

across.definition: A word in a puzzle that goes from left to right. You write it in a line going sideways.
across.exampleSentence: I can do the first across in this puzzle.
across.contextPassage: In a word puzzle, across means going sideways. It is the opposite of down.
distractors: A word in a puzzle that goes from top to bottom. You write it in a line going down.
A number that tells you how hard the puzzle is. A big number is very hard.
A box in a puzzle that is already filled in. You do not write in it.

actor.definition: A person who plays a character in a film or show. They pretend to be someone else.
actor.exampleSentence: The actor is in a big new film.
actor.contextPassage: An actor can work in films or on a stage. Many people watch and listen to the actor.
distractors: A person who makes films and tells everyone what to do. They are the boss on set.
A person who writes the words for films and shows. The actor then says these words.
A person who sings songs on a big stage. Many people come to listen to them.

actress.definition: A woman or girl who plays a character in a film or show. She pretends to be someone else.
actress.exampleSentence: The actress is very good in this film.
actress.contextPassage: An actress works in films or on a stage. She says the words and acts out the story.
distractors: A woman who makes films and tells everyone what to do. She is the boss on set.
A woman who writes the words for films and shows. The actress then says her words.
A woman who dances in a big show. Many people come to watch her dance.

add.definition: When you put one number with another number. You get a bigger number.
add.exampleSentence: I do an add with five and three.
add.contextPassage: An add gives you a new, bigger number. You use plus to do an add.
distractors: When you take one number away from another. You get a smaller number.
When you cut a big number into two small numbers. Both numbers are the same.
When you put the same number many times together. You do this very fast.

address.definition: The name of the place where you live. It tells people where to find your home.
address.exampleSentence: My address is on this letter.
address.contextPassage: You write your address on letters and cards. People need your address to send you things.
distractors: A special number for your phone. People use it to call you.
A name you use on the internet. People see it when you write online.
A small card with your name on it. You give it to new people you meet.

afraid.definition: You feel this when something is scary. Your body feels bad and you want to run.
afraid.exampleSentence: I am afraid of big dogs.
afraid.contextPassage: You can feel afraid of the dark or loud sounds. It is not a good feeling.
distractors: You feel this when you are very happy. You smile and want to jump.
You feel this when you are very tired. You want to sleep in your bed.
You feel this when you are angry about something. You do not like it at all.

after.definition: Coming next. It is the one that comes second, not first.
after.contextPassage: After means something is next in order. It comes when the first thing is done.
distractors: Coming before everything else. It is the very first one, not second.
Being the same as something else. The two things come at the same time.
Being in the middle. One thing comes before it and one comes after it.

afternoon.definition: The part of the day after lunch. It goes until it gets dark outside.
afternoon.exampleSentence: We play in the park in the afternoon.
afternoon.contextPassage: Afternoon comes after midday. School often ends in the afternoon.
distractors: The part of the day before breakfast. The sun is just coming up in the sky.
The part of the day when it is dark and you are in bed. You sleep at this time.
The part of the day just before lunch. You eat your morning meal at this time.

again.definition: One more time. You do the same thing you did before.
again.contextPassage: You do something again when you want to repeat it. You go back and do it one more time.
distractors: For the very first time. You have never done this thing before.
At the same time as someone else. You both do it together right now.
After a very long time. You wait many years before you do it.

age.definition: How many years old you are.
age.exampleSentence: My age is seven years old.
age.contextPassage: You can ask a friend about their age. People have different ages.
distractors: How tall a person is.
How much a person weighs.
The name of the place you live in.

ago.definition: A time before now, in the past.
ago.exampleSentence: I ate lunch two hours ago.
ago.contextPassage: We use ago to talk about the past. For example, we say one day ago or two years ago.
distractors: A time that will come soon.
Right now, at this moment.
A very long time from today.

air.definition: The thing all around us that we breathe in.
air.exampleSentence: I breathe clean air every day.
air.contextPassage: Air is everywhere around you. You cannot see air, but you need it to live.
distractors: The water we drink to stay alive.
The food we eat to feel good.
The ground we walk and stand on.

airport.definition: A big place where planes go up and land.
airport.exampleSentence: We go to the airport for our trip.
airport.contextPassage: At an airport, you can see many big planes. People go there to travel to other places.
distractors: A big place where trains stop and go.
A place where big boats come and stop.
A place where cars go fast in a race.

all.definition: Every part of something, nothing left out.
all.exampleSentence: I eat all my food at dinner.
all.contextPassage: All means everything together, not just one part. For example, all the children are here.
distractors: Just one small part of something.
Only two things from a group.
The very last thing in a group.

also.definition: Too, as well, one more thing is true.
also.exampleSentence: I like cats and I also like dogs.
also.contextPassage: We use also to add more information. It means something is true in addition to what we said.
distractors: Only this one thing is true, not more.
This happened before everything else.
This is the last and only thing.

always.definition: Every time, it never stops or changes.
always.exampleSentence: She always drinks water in the morning.
always.contextPassage: Always means it happens every day, not just one time. For example, he always goes to school.
distractors: Something that happens only one time.
Something that happens only sometimes.
Something that will never happen again.

and.definition: A word that joins two things together.
and.exampleSentence: I have a cat and a dog.
and.contextPassage: We use and to connect two words or ideas. For example, bread and water go together.
distractors: A word that means you cannot have two things.
A word that shows something is very big.
A word that means you choose only one thing.

angry.definition: You feel very bad and not happy inside.
angry.exampleSentence: He is angry because his toy is gone.
angry.contextPassage: People feel angry when something bad happens. When you are angry, you do not feel calm or happy.
distractors: You feel very happy and you want to play.
You feel very tired and you want to sleep.
You feel very scared about something bad.

animal.definition: A living thing that can move, like a dog or cat.
animal.exampleSentence: A dog is my favourite animal.
animal.contextPassage: Animals can walk, swim, or fly. They eat food and they can feel things.
distractors: A living thing that grows in the ground and cannot move.
A thing people make to help them do work.
A very small living thing that you can only see with help.

another.definition: One more of the same thing.
another.exampleSentence: Can I have another apple, please?
another.contextPassage: We use another when we want one more. For example, she drinks another glass of water.
distractors: The very first one of something.
The last one left of something.
A thing that is very different from this one.

answer.definition: What you say or write back to a question.
answer.exampleSentence: She knows the answer to the question.
answer.contextPassage: A teacher asks a question and you give an answer. A good answer helps someone understand something.
distractors: A question you ask when you do not know something.
A story you tell to make people happy.
A word you say to greet a friend.

any.definition: Even a little bit. Used to ask if something is true at all.
any.exampleSentence: Do you have any water for me?
any.contextPassage: We use 'any' in questions and sentences with 'not'. For example: I do not have any food.
distractors: A very big amount of something.
All of something together.
Only one thing, not more.

anyone.definition: Any person at all. One person, but we do not know who.
anyone.contextPassage: We use 'anyone' in questions and with 'not'. For example: I do not see anyone here.
distractors: A thing, not a person.
A group of many people together.
A person you know very well.

anything.definition: Any thing at all. One thing, but we do not know what.
anything.exampleSentence: I do not want anything to eat now.
anything.contextPassage: We use 'anything' in questions and with 'not'. For example: Do you need anything from the shop?
distractors: A person, not a thing.
A place you can go to.
Many things all together.

apartment.definition: A home inside a big building. Other people live in the same building too.
apartment.exampleSentence: I live in a small apartment in the city.
apartment.contextPassage: An apartment has a door, a kitchen, and a bedroom. Many apartments are in one big building.
distractors: A big house with a garden outside.
A room in a hotel for one night.
A small building for one family only.

apple.definition: A round fruit. It can be red, green, or yellow. It is good to eat.
apple.exampleSentence: I eat one apple every day at school.
apple.contextPassage: An apple grows on a tree. It is sweet and good for you.
distractors: A yellow fruit with soft inside. It grows in hot places.
A small red fruit. It is very sweet and small.
A round orange fruit. You can drink its juice.

april.definition: The fourth month of the year. It comes after March.
april.exampleSentence: My birthday is in April this year.
april.contextPassage: April has thirty days. In many places, it is warm in April.
distractors: The third month of the year. It comes after February.
The fifth month of the year. It comes after April.
The second month of the year. It is a short month.

arm.definition: The part of your body from your shoulder to your hand.
arm.exampleSentence: My arm hurts after I carry the big bag.
arm.contextPassage: You have two arms. You use your arms to carry things and to hug people.
distractors: The part of your body you walk and run with.
The part of your face you use to see things.
The part of your body at the top of your back.

around.definition: Near this place. Close to here, not far away.
around.exampleSentence: Is your teacher around today at school?
around.contextPassage: We say 'around' to mean someone is near. For example: My friend is not around today.
distractors: Very far away from this place.
Inside a small closed room.
On top of something high up.

arrive.definition: To get to a place. You go there and then you are there.
arrive.exampleSentence: We arrive at school at eight o'clock.
arrive.contextPassage: You can arrive at home, at school, or at the shop. The opposite of arrive is leave.
distractors: To go away from a place and not come back.
To look for a place but not find it.
To stop and wait in one place for a long time.

art.definition: A picture, drawing, or painting that someone makes. It looks beautiful.
art.exampleSentence: I see beautiful art on the wall at school.
art.contextPassage: You can make art with paint or pencils. Art can be a picture or a drawing.
distractors: Words in a book that tell a story.
Music you listen to with your ears.
A dance you do with your body and feet.

artist.definition: A person who makes pictures, drawings, or paintings.
artist.exampleSentence: The artist makes a big and beautiful painting today.
artist.contextPassage: An artist uses paint, pencils, or other things to make art. Some artists work in a big room called a studio.
distractors: A person who plays music for other people.
A person who writes stories in books.
A person who dances in front of other people.

as.definition: The same amount or the same way. Used to compare two things.
as.exampleSentence: This bag is as big as that one.
as.contextPassage: We use 'as' to say two things are the same. For example: She is as tall as me.
distractors: More than something else. Not the same, but bigger.
A little bit, but not very much at all.
Less than something else. Not as much or big.

ask.definition: When you want something, you say words to get it. This is an ask.
ask.exampleSentence: That is a big ask for me.
ask.contextPassage: You make an ask when you need help. It is a request from you to someone.
distractors: A time when you say thank you to someone.
A time when you give something to a friend.
A time when you tell someone to stop talking.

at.definition: To write or say something to a person, online or in real life.
at.exampleSentence: She ats me on the app every day.
at.contextPassage: You can at someone in a message online. You write their name to talk to them.
distractors: To read a message from someone online.
To send a photo to a friend.
To close an app on your phone.

august.definition: Very big and important. People look at this person or thing with great respect.
august.exampleSentence: The old king is an august man.
august.contextPassage: You can say a place or person is august. It means they are very great and people respect them.
distractors: Very small and not important to people.
Very funny and happy all the time.
Very busy and always moving fast.

aunt.definition: Your mother or father has a sister. She is your aunt.
aunt.exampleSentence: My aunt has a big house.
aunt.contextPassage: Your aunt is family. She is the sister of your mum or dad.
distractors: The mother of your mother or father.
The daughter of your mother or father.
The wife of your mother or father.

autumn.definition: A time of year when it gets cold and leaves fall from trees.
autumn.exampleSentence: The trees are red in autumn.
autumn.contextPassage: Autumn comes after summer. The weather gets cold and leaves fall down.
distractors: A time of year when it is very hot and sunny.
A time of year when trees get new green leaves.
A time of year when it snows and is very cold.

away.definition: To go from a place to another place. To leave.
away.contextPassage: You away when you leave a place. You go to another place.
distractors: To come to a new place for the first time.
To sit down and rest in one place.
To look for something in your home.

baby.definition: A very young child. It cannot walk or talk yet.
baby.exampleSentence: The baby is very small and cute.
baby.contextPassage: A baby is very young. It drinks milk and sleeps a lot.
distractors: A child who goes to school every day.
A young person who can run and play.
A person who is ten years old.

back.definition: The part of your body behind you. It is between your neck and your bottom.
back.exampleSentence: My back hurts after the long walk.
back.contextPassage: Your back is on the other side from your chest. You cannot see your own back easily.
distractors: The part of your body at the front, below your neck.
The part of your body at the top, above your neck.
The part of your arm below your hand.

bad.definition: Something you do that is not correct. You make a bad.
bad.contextPassage: You say my bad when you do something wrong. It means you made a mistake.
distractors: Something very good that you do well.
A new idea you want to try.
A question you ask a friend.

bag.definition: A soft thing you put your things in. You can carry it with you.
bag.exampleSentence: I put my book in my bag.
bag.contextPassage: A bag can be big or small. You use it to carry things with you.
distractors: A hard box you keep things in at home.
A small flat thing you write on.
A big table you put food on.

ball.definition: A round thing you can throw, kick, or catch. It can be big or small.
ball.exampleSentence: The children kick the ball in the park.
ball.contextPassage: A ball is round. You use it to play games.
distractors: A long flat thing you hit with your hand.
A small hard thing you use to write.
A thin round thing you eat for breakfast.

banana.definition: A long yellow fruit. It is sweet and soft inside.
banana.exampleSentence: I eat a banana for breakfast.
banana.contextPassage: A banana is yellow when it is ready to eat. It has a soft white inside.
distractors: A small red fruit that grows on a tree.
A big orange fruit with a hard skin.
A long green vegetable you cook and eat.

band.definition: A thin piece of something. It holds things together.
band.exampleSentence: She puts a band in her hair.
band.contextPassage: A band can be made of rubber or cloth. It keeps things from moving apart.
distractors: A sharp tool you use to cut food or paper.
A long, thin string you use to tie a box.
A small button you press to open a door.

bank.definition: A place where people keep their money safe.
bank.exampleSentence: My dad goes to the bank today.
bank.contextPassage: You can put money in the bank. You can also take money out of the bank.
distractors: A big shop where you buy food and drinks.
A place where doctors help sick people.
A school where children go to learn.

bath.definition: A big container you fill with water to wash your body.
bath.exampleSentence: I sit in the bath every evening.
bath.contextPassage: You use warm water in the bath. It is a good way to get clean.
distractors: A small cloth you use to dry your hands.
A place outside where you wash your clothes.
A small cup you use to drink water.

bathroom.definition: A room in a house with a bath or shower inside.
bathroom.exampleSentence: The bathroom is next to my bedroom.
bathroom.contextPassage: You go to the bathroom to wash. There is often a toilet in the bathroom too.
distractors: A room where you eat food with your family.
A room with a big table where you do work.
A room where you keep your coats and shoes.

be.definition: A word that tells us about something or someone.
be.contextPassage: We use this word to say how something is. For example, the sky is blue.
distractors: A word that means you want to eat something.
A word that means you move from one place to another.
A word that means you can see something far away.

beach.definition: A place next to the sea with sand or small stones.
beach.exampleSentence: We play at the beach in summer.
beach.contextPassage: The beach is next to the water. Many people go there on hot days.
distractors: A big area of trees where animals live.
A flat, green area of grass in a park.
A high place made of rock and stone.

beautiful.definition: Very, very nice to look at.
beautiful.exampleSentence: She has a beautiful dress today.
beautiful.contextPassage: We say something is beautiful when we love how it looks. Flowers and sunsets can be beautiful.
distractors: Very loud and easy to hear from far away.
Very big and heavy and hard to move.
Very fast and good at running.

because.definition: A word that tells you the reason for something.
because.exampleSentence: I am happy because it is sunny.
because.contextPassage: We use this word to explain why something happens. It comes after the thing you want to explain.
distractors: A word that means something happens two times.
A word that means something is very far away.
A word that means you are not sure about something.

bed.definition: A soft, flat thing you sleep on at night.
bed.exampleSentence: I go to bed at nine o'clock.
bed.contextPassage: A bed is in your bedroom. It is soft and comfortable to sleep on.
distractors: A big, soft seat you sit on in the living room.
A long table you use to eat your food.
A small chair you sit on at school.

bedroom.definition: A room in a house where you sleep at night.
bedroom.exampleSentence: My bedroom has a big window.
bedroom.contextPassage: Your bed is in the bedroom. Many people also keep their clothes in the bedroom.
distractors: A room where you cook and make food.
A room where you sit and watch TV with family.
A room where you eat meals with other people.

beer.definition: A cold drink that has alcohol in it.
beer.exampleSentence: My dad drinks a cold beer after work.
beer.contextPassage: Beer is a drink for adults. It is often yellow or brown.
distractors: A sweet, cold drink made from fruit and water.
A hot drink made from dark brown beans.
A cold drink with many small bubbles and no alcohol.

before.definition: At a time in the past, not now.
before.exampleSentence: I wash my hands before I eat.
before.contextPassage: We use this word when one thing happens first. It comes before another thing in time.
distractors: A word that means something happens at the same time.
A word that means something is very close to you.
A word that means something happens again and again.

behind.definition: The back part of something. It is not the front.
behind.exampleSentence: The dog is behind the chair.
behind.contextPassage: We use 'behind' to say where something is. It means at the back.
distractors: The front part of something. It is not the back.
The part on the left side of something.
The top part of something. It is up high.

below.definition: In a lower place. It is not up high.
below.exampleSentence: The fish is below the water.
below.contextPassage: We use 'below' to say something is under something else. It is a place word.
distractors: In a higher place. It is up above.
Next to something on the right side.
Far away from you. You can not see it.

best.definition: The most good you can do. You try very hard.
best.exampleSentence: She does her best at school.
best.contextPassage: We say 'do your best' when you try as hard as you can. It means you give all you have.
distractors: A small try. You do not work hard.
Something you do very fast but not well.
The first time you try something new.

better.definition: A person who is more good than another person.
better.exampleSentence: She is a better swimmer than me.
better.contextPassage: We use 'better' to say one person is more good than another. It is about two people.
distractors: A person who is the same as you. You are equal.
A person who is not as good as you.
A person you do not know at all.

between.definition: A small tool with a hole. You use it to sew thick cloth.
between.exampleSentence: She uses a between to sew her bag.
between.contextPassage: A 'between' is a short needle. It has a small round hole at one end.
distractors: A long sharp tool. You use it to sew thin cloth.
A flat tool. You use it to cut cloth.
A small tool. You use it to pin cloth together.

bicycle.definition: A thing you ride. It has two wheels and you move your feet to go.
bicycle.exampleSentence: I ride my bicycle to school.
bicycle.contextPassage: A bicycle has two wheels and a seat. You use your feet on the pedals to move.
distractors: A thing with four wheels. You sit inside and a motor moves it.
A big thing with many wheels. Many people sit inside it.
A thing with two wheels. A motor moves it and you do not use your feet.

big.definition: A person or thing that is very large. It is not small.
big.exampleSentence: He is a big boy in the class.
big.contextPassage: We use 'big' for a person or thing that is large. The opposite of big is small.
distractors: A person or thing that is very small. It is not large.
A person or thing that is very fast. It moves quickly.
A person or thing that is very old. It has lived a long time.

bike.definition: A thing you ride. It has two wheels and you use your feet to move it.
bike.exampleSentence: He rides his bike in the park.
bike.contextPassage: A bike has two wheels and a seat. You sit on it and move your feet to go.
distractors: A thing with four wheels. A motor inside it makes it go.
A big thing on water. It takes you from place to place.
A thing with wings. It goes up high in the sky.

bill.definition: A heavy weapon with a sharp blade on a long stick. Old soldiers used it to fight.
bill.exampleSentence: The old soldier had a bill in his hand.
bill.contextPassage: A bill is an old weapon. People used it to fight a long time ago.
distractors: A small weapon you hold in one hand. It is short and has a sharp point.
A long weapon you throw at the enemy. It flies through the air.
A weapon you pull back and shoot. It sends small objects very fast.

bird.definition: A small animal with wings and feathers. It can fly in the sky.
bird.exampleSentence: I see a bird in the tree.
bird.contextPassage: A bird has wings and feathers. It lays eggs and can fly.
distractors: A small animal with four legs. It lives in the water and on land.
A big animal with four legs. It eats grass in the field.
A small animal with no legs. It moves on its belly on the ground.

birthday.definition: The day you were born. It comes one time every year.
birthday.exampleSentence: Today is my birthday. I am happy!
birthday.contextPassage: We say happy birthday to a person on their special day. Friends and family give you a gift.
distractors: A special day when your family eats a big meal together.
A day when you go to school for the first time.
A special day at the end of every year. People are happy and sing.

black.definition: A very dark colour. You see it when there is no light.
black.exampleSentence: The night sky is black and dark.
black.contextPassage: Black is a colour. It is the darkest colour you can see.
distractors: A very light colour. It is the colour of snow and milk.
A bright colour. It is the colour of the sun and bananas.
A dark colour. It is the colour of the sky on a sunny day.

blog.definition: A place on the internet where a person writes about their life or ideas.
blog.exampleSentence: She writes a blog about food.
blog.contextPassage: People read blogs to find new ideas. You can write a comment on a blog.
distractors: A small device you use to take photos.
A shop where you can buy books.
A TV show about news and people.

blonde.definition: A light yellow color, like the sun. People say this about hair.
blonde.contextPassage: Blonde hair is a very light color. It is not brown and not white.
distractors: Hair that is very dark, like the night sky.
Hair that is red, like fire.
Hair that is grey, like clouds.

blue.definition: A color like the sky on a sunny day or the deep sea.
blue.contextPassage: Blue is a very common color. Water in pictures is often blue.
distractors: A color like grass and trees in summer.
A color like the sun or a lemon.
A color like an apple or a stop sign.

boat.definition: A small vehicle that moves on water.
boat.exampleSentence: We go on a boat on the lake.
boat.contextPassage: People use a boat to travel on water. A small boat can go on a river or a lake.
distractors: A big vehicle that flies in the sky.
A vehicle with four wheels that goes on roads.
A vehicle on metal tracks that carries many people.

body.definition: All the parts of a person or animal, like arms, legs, and head.
body.exampleSentence: My body is tired after the game.
body.contextPassage: You use your body to move and do things. Arms and legs are parts of the body.
distractors: The thoughts and feelings inside your head.
The clothes you put on every day.
The food you eat to stay strong.

book.definition: Many pages with words or pictures. You can read it.
book.exampleSentence: I read a good book today.
book.contextPassage: A book has a front cover and a back cover. You can find books in a library or a shop.
distractors: A big screen where you watch films or shows.
A small card with a person's name and number on it.
A piece of paper where you write a short note.

boot.definition: A big shoe that covers your foot and part of your leg.
boot.exampleSentence: She puts on her boots in the rain.
boot.contextPassage: People wear boots when it is cold or wet outside. Boots are big and strong shoes.
distractors: A light shoe you wear inside the house.
A small, open shoe you wear in summer.
A sock you put on before your shoes.

bored.definition: The feeling you have when nothing is fun or interesting.
bored.exampleSentence: He is bored at home today.
bored.contextPassage: You feel bored when you have nothing to do. A long wait can make you feel bored.
distractors: The feeling you have when you are very happy and excited.
The feeling you have when you are scared of something.
The feeling you have when you are very tired and want to sleep.

boring.definition: Not fun or interesting at all.
boring.contextPassage: A boring thing does not make you happy or excited. People do not like boring things.
distractors: Very funny and good to watch or listen to.
Very hard and difficult to do or understand.
Very loud and not nice to hear.

born.definition: When a baby comes out into the world and starts life.
born.exampleSentence: She was born in a small town.
born.contextPassage: Every person is born on a day. That day is your birthday.
distractors: When a person is very old and stops living.
When a child starts to go to school for the first time.
When a person moves to a new home in a new place.

both.definition: Two things or two people, not just one. All two of them.
both.exampleSentence: Both my sisters are at school.
both.contextPassage: You say both when you talk about two things together. Both hands, both eyes, both friends.
distractors: Not one thing and not the other thing. Zero of them.
Only one of the two things, not the other one.
A very big number of things, more than ten.

bottle.definition: A tall container for water, juice, or other drinks. It has a small top.
bottle.exampleSentence: I drink water from a bottle.
bottle.contextPassage: A bottle can be made of glass or plastic. You can close a bottle with a small top called a cap.
distractors: A flat dish that you put food on to eat.
A deep bowl that you use to drink hot tea or coffee.
A big box where you keep food cold and fresh.

box.definition: A thing with four sides, a bottom, and a top. You put things inside it.
box.exampleSentence: I put my toys in the box.
box.contextPassage: A box can be big or small. You can open a box to see what is inside.
distractors: A flat thing you write on. You use it at school.
A small bag you carry with you. It has a handle.
A round thing you can kick or throw. It is a toy.

boy.definition: A young male person. He is not a girl.
boy.exampleSentence: The boy plays in the park.
boy.contextPassage: A boy can be a child or a young man. Boys and girls go to school together.
distractors: A young female person. She is not a boy.
An old male person. He has grey hair.
A very small baby. It cannot walk or talk.

boyfriend.definition: A man that a woman loves. They are together but not married.
boyfriend.contextPassage: A boyfriend is a special person to you. You like to spend time with your boyfriend.
distractors: A man in your family. He is your father or brother.
A good friend at school. You play with him every day.
A man you work with. You see him at your job.

bread.definition: A soft food you can eat. It is made and cooked in an oven.
bread.exampleSentence: I eat bread for lunch every day.
bread.contextPassage: Bread can be white or brown. You can put butter on bread.
distractors: A sweet food you eat after dinner. It is cold and soft.
A drink that is white. It comes from a cow.
A yellow food you cook in a pan. It comes from a chicken.

break.definition: When something goes into two or more pieces. It is not one piece now.
break.exampleSentence: There is a break in the plate.
break.contextPassage: A break happens when something falls or hits a hard thing. After a break, the thing is not the same.
distractors: When you cut something with a knife. You make it smaller.
When you open something. You can see what is inside.
When you bend something. It goes a different way.

breakfast.definition: The first meal you eat in the day. You eat it in the morning.
breakfast.exampleSentence: I eat eggs for breakfast every morning.
breakfast.contextPassage: Breakfast is good for you. Many people eat bread or fruit for breakfast.
distractors: The meal you eat at the middle of the day. You are at school or work.
The meal you eat in the evening. You eat it at home with family.
A small meal you eat between two big meals. You eat it when you are a little hungry.

bring.definition: To carry something to a place or to a person.
bring.exampleSentence: Please bring me a glass of water.
bring.contextPassage: You bring something with you when you go to a place. You can bring food or a gift to a friend.
distractors: To put something down and walk away from it. You do not take it with you.
To find something that is not with you. You look for it in a new place.
To give something to a person and not take it back. It is their thing now.

brother.definition: A boy or man with the same mother and father as you.
brother.exampleSentence: My brother is ten years old.
brother.contextPassage: A brother is part of your family. You and your brother have the same mum and dad.
distractors: A girl or woman with the same mother and father as you.
The son of your aunt or uncle. He is part of your family too.
The man your mother or father is married to. He lives with your family.

brown.definition: A colour like chocolate or coffee. It is a dark warm colour.
brown.contextPassage: Brown is the colour of wood and soil. Many animals are brown.
distractors: A colour like the sky on a sunny day. It is a cool light colour.
A colour like grass and trees. It is a cool dark colour.
A colour like the sun or a lemon. It is a bright warm colour.

build.definition: The size and shape of a person's body. It shows if they are big, small, or strong.
build.exampleSentence: He has a big and strong build.
build.contextPassage: You can say a person has a small or large build. A build tells you what a body looks like.
distractors: The colour of a person's skin or hair. It is what you see on the outside.
How tall a person is. You can see this when they stand up.
How heavy a person is. You can see the number on a scale.

building.definition: A big structure with walls and a roof. People go inside it.
building.exampleSentence: The school is a very big building.
building.contextPassage: A building can be a house, a shop, or a hospital. People live or work inside a building.
distractors: A long road that goes from one place to another. Cars go on it.
A tall structure with no walls. You can see it from far away.
A small place outside with no roof. People sit there in the sun.

bus.definition: A very big vehicle. Many people can sit inside and go to places.
bus.exampleSentence: I go to school on the bus.
bus.contextPassage: A bus stops at many places on the road. You pay to ride on a bus.
distractors: A vehicle that goes on water. It is very big and slow.
A small vehicle for one or two people. You call it when you need a ride.
A vehicle that goes very fast in the sky. It takes you to other countries.

busy.definition: A person whose job is to keep people safe. This person works for the police.
busy.exampleSentence: The busy helps people in the street.
busy.contextPassage: A busy wears a uniform. You can talk to a busy if you need help.
distractors: A person whose job is to help sick people feel better.
A person whose job is to teach children at school.
A person whose job is to put out fires.

but.definition: A time when someone says the word but in a sentence.
but.exampleSentence: He said a big but in his answer.
but.contextPassage: A but shows two different ideas. You use a but to say something different next.
distractors: A time when someone says yes to something.
A word that shows two things are the same.
A word you use to add more of the same idea.

butter.definition: A soft, yellow food made from milk. You put it on bread.
butter.exampleSentence: I put butter on my bread this morning.
butter.contextPassage: Butter is cold and hard in the fridge. It gets soft when it is warm.
distractors: A sweet, thick food that bees make. It is yellow and very sweet.
A soft white food made from milk. It is cold and you eat it with fruit.
A drink made from milk. It is white and good for you.

buy.definition: A thing that you get when you pay money for it.
buy.exampleSentence: That shirt was a good buy for me.
buy.contextPassage: A good buy means you pay a small price. You get a good buy at a shop sale.
distractors: A thing that someone gives you with no money.
A thing you make at home with your hands.
A thing you find on the street for free.

by.definition: A small insect that flies. It makes honey and can sting you.
by.exampleSentence: A by is sitting on that flower.
by.contextPassage: A by collects food from flowers. Some bys make honey that we eat.
distractors: A small insect that flies at night and is near lights.
A small green insect that lives in the grass and jumps high.
A small insect with big wings. It is very beautiful and has bright colours.

bye exclam..definition: You say this word when you leave someone. It means goodbye.
bye exclam..exampleSentence: She said bye and left the room.
bye exclam..contextPassage: People say bye to friends at the end of the day. It is a friendly word.
distractors: You say this when you see someone for the first time today.
You say this when you want someone to come to you.
You say this when you say thank you to someone.

cafe.definition: A small shop where you can buy drinks and simple food.
cafe.exampleSentence: We drink coffee at the cafe every day.
cafe.contextPassage: A cafe has tables and chairs inside. You can sit and eat or drink there.
distractors: A big shop where you buy food to take home and cook.
A place where you sit and watch people cook food for you at night.
A small shop where you buy sweet things like cake and bread to take home.

cake.definition: A sweet food. You bake it in an oven with flour, eggs, and sugar.
cake.exampleSentence: She has a big cake for her birthday.
cake.contextPassage: People eat cake at parties. Cake is sweet and very good.
distractors: A sweet food you eat cold. It is made from milk and is very soft.
A small sweet food. You fry it in oil and eat it in the morning.
A hard sweet food. You eat it with your hands and it is very small.

call.definition: A talk you have with someone on the phone.
call.exampleSentence: I have a call with my friend at noon.
call.contextPassage: You make a call on your phone. A call can be long or short.
distractors: A short message you write and send to someone on your phone.
A letter you write on paper and send to someone far away.
A talk you have with someone in the same room.

camera.definition: A thing you use to take photos or videos.
camera.exampleSentence: She has a new camera for her trip.
camera.contextPassage: You point the camera at something you like. Then you take a photo of it.
distractors: A small screen you use to watch videos and talk to people.
A small tool you use to record only sound, not pictures.
A big screen on the wall. You watch films on it.

can.definition: To be able to do something. You know how to do it.
can.exampleSentence: I can swim very well in the sea.
can.contextPassage: You say can before another verb. It shows you are able to do that thing.
distractors: To want to do something very much right now.
To do something every day because you have to.
To try to do something but not be able to finish it.

cannot.definition: A thing that is not possible for someone to do.
cannot.exampleSentence: Flying is a cannot for people without wings.
cannot.contextPassage: A cannot shows something is not possible. You use cannot to say no to an action.
distractors: A thing that is very easy for everyone to do.
A thing you do every day without thinking about it.
A thing you can do only with help from another person.

car.definition: A machine with four wheels. It can take you from one place to another. A person drives it.
car.exampleSentence: My dad drives a red car to work.
car.contextPassage: A car needs petrol to go. You can sit four or five people in a car.
distractors: A big machine that moves on metal tracks and carries many people from city to city.
A machine that flies in the sky and carries people to far places.
A machine with two wheels. You use your legs to make it go.

card.definition: A small, flat piece of paper with numbers and pictures on it. You use it to play games.
card.exampleSentence: She has a red card in her hand.
card.contextPassage: You can play many games with cards. A card can have a number or a picture on it.
distractors: A small, flat square made of wood. You use it to build things.
A long piece of paper. You write a letter on it and send it to a friend.
A small plastic square. You use it to pay for things in a shop.

carrot.definition: A long, orange vegetable. It grows in the ground and is good to eat.
carrot.exampleSentence: I eat a carrot with my lunch today.
carrot.contextPassage: A carrot is orange and long. You can eat it raw or cook it in hot water.
distractors: A round, red vegetable. It grows on a plant above the ground.
A small, green vegetable. It grows in the ground and is very small and round.
A big, orange vegetable. It is round and you can make soup with it.

carry.definition: The way you hold something to take it with you. For example, in your arms or hands.
carry.exampleSentence: She has a good carry for the big box.
carry.contextPassage: You can carry a bag in your hands. The carry can be easy or hard.
distractors: The way you put something down on a table or on the floor.
The way you throw something to another person with your hand.
The way you push something along the ground with your foot.

cat.definition: A small, soft animal with four legs. It lives in your home and says meow.
cat.exampleSentence: My cat sleeps on my bed.
cat.contextPassage: A cat is a good pet for your home. It likes to eat fish and drink milk.
distractors: A small animal with long ears. It is soft and white and lives in a garden.
A big animal with four legs. It says woof and it loves to run and play.
A small animal with wings. It can fly and it makes a nice sound.

cent.definition: A very small amount of money. One hundred cents make one dollar or euro.
cent.exampleSentence: This sweet costs fifty cents at the shop.
cent.contextPassage: A cent is a very small coin. You need one hundred cents to make one big unit of money.
distractors: A big coin. It is the main unit of money in many countries.
A piece of paper money. You use it to buy big things in a shop.
A small card. You use it to pay for things without coins or paper money.

centre.definition: The middle point of something. It is the same distance from all the sides.
centre.exampleSentence: The ball is in the centre of the circle.
centre.contextPassage: The centre is the middle of a shape or a place. It is not near any side or edge.
distractors: The outside edge of a round shape. It goes all the way around the middle.
The top part of something. It is the highest point of an object.
The corner of a shape. It is where two sides of a shape come together.

chair.definition: A piece of furniture with four legs and a back. One person sits on it.
chair.exampleSentence: I sit on a chair at the table.
chair.contextPassage: A chair has a seat and a back. You can find chairs in your home, at school, and in restaurants.
distractors: A long piece of furniture. Two or three people can sit on it together.
A soft piece of furniture. You can lie down and sleep on it.
A small piece of furniture with no back. You sit on the top part of it.

change.definition: When something is not the same as before. It is different now.
change.exampleSentence: I can see a big change in the weather today.
change.contextPassage: Change happens when something is new or different. You can see change in people, places, and things.
distractors: When something stays the same and does not move or look different.
When something breaks and you cannot use it anymore.
When something gets very old and you put it away.

cheap.definition: Something that does not cost a lot of money. It is easy to buy.
cheap.exampleSentence: This small bag is very cheap at the market.
cheap.contextPassage: A cheap thing costs very little money. You can buy cheap things when you do not have a lot of money.
distractors: Something that costs a lot of money. Not many people can buy it.
Something that is very good and strong. It lasts for a very long time.
Something that is free. You do not pay any money for it.

check.definition: In a game of chess, your king is in danger. The other player's piece is very close to it.
check.exampleSentence: He says check and my king is in danger now.
check.contextPassage: Check happens in the game of chess. You must move your king fast or it is in big danger.
distractors: When you win the game of chess and the other player cannot move at all.
When you start a new game of chess and put all the pieces on the board.
When you take one of the other player's pieces off the board in chess.

cheese.definition: A food made from milk. It is soft or hard and you can eat it in many ways.
cheese.exampleSentence: I put cheese on my bread for lunch.
cheese.contextPassage: Cheese comes from milk. It can be soft, hard, yellow, or white.
distractors: A white drink that comes from cows. It is cold and good for you.
A cold food made from milk and sugar. It is sweet and you eat it with a spoon.
A yellow food that comes from milk. You put it on hot bread and it melts.
A thick, white food made from milk. It is cold and you eat it for breakfast.

chicken.definition: A farm bird. People eat its meat and eggs.
chicken.exampleSentence: I eat chicken and rice for lunch.
chicken.contextPassage: Chicken is a very common food. You can cook it in many ways.
distractors: A big animal that lives in water and has fins.
A small animal with soft hair. It lives in your home.
A farm animal with four legs. It gives us milk.

child.definition: A young person. Not a baby and not an adult.
child.exampleSentence: The child plays in the park every day.
child.contextPassage: A child is bigger than a baby. A child goes to school to learn.
distractors: A very small person who cannot walk yet.
A person who is very old and has a big family.
A person who works and has their own home.

chocolate.definition: A sweet brown food. It is made from special beans.
chocolate.exampleSentence: I eat chocolate as a treat after dinner.
chocolate.contextPassage: Chocolate can be a drink or a bar of food. Many people like chocolate because it is very sweet.
distractors: A cold sweet food made from milk and fruit.
A small round sweet with sugar on the outside.
A soft sweet food made from bread and honey.

choose.definition: To pick one thing from two or more things.
choose.exampleSentence: I choose the red apple from the bowl.
choose.contextPassage: You choose when you have more than one option. For example, you choose food from a menu.
distractors: To say no to something you do not want.
To take something that belongs to another person.
To ask someone to give you something.

cinema.definition: A big room where you go to watch films.
cinema.exampleSentence: We go to the cinema on Friday night.
cinema.contextPassage: At the cinema, you sit in a big dark room. You watch a film on a very large screen.
distractors: A place where you go to listen to live music.
A place where you go to read books and be quiet.
A big room where you play sport and exercise.

city.definition: A very big place where many people live and work.
city.exampleSentence: London is a very big and busy city.
city.contextPassage: A city has many buildings, shops, and roads. It is bigger than a town or a village.
distractors: A small quiet place with only a few houses.
A place with no people and many trees and animals.
A group of houses near water by the sea.

class.definition: A group of students who learn together.
class.exampleSentence: My class has twenty students in it.
class.contextPassage: You are in a class at school. Your teacher talks to your class every day.
distractors: A small room where one student reads alone.
A big test that students do at the end of school.
A book that a teacher uses to write notes.

classroom.definition: A room in a school where students learn.
classroom.exampleSentence: We sit at desks in the classroom.
classroom.contextPassage: A classroom has chairs and a board. The teacher stands at the front of the classroom.
distractors: A big room where students eat lunch at school.
A room in a school where students play sport inside.
A quiet room in a school where students read books alone.

clean.definition: To remove all the dirt from something.
clean.exampleSentence: I clean my room every Saturday morning.
clean.contextPassage: You clean something to make it not dirty. You can clean with water and soap.
distractors: To make something very cold and put it away.
To break something into many small pieces.
To make something a new color with paint.

climb.definition: To go up something, like a tree or a hill.
climb.exampleSentence: The boy climbs up the big tree.
climb.contextPassage: You use your hands and feet to climb. You can climb stairs, a wall, or a hill.
distractors: To move fast on the ground using your legs.
To go from a high place down to a low place.
To jump over something that is in your way.

clock.definition: A thing that shows you what time it is.
clock.exampleSentence: The clock on the wall says two o'clock.
clock.contextPassage: A clock is on a wall or a table. You look at the clock to know the time.
distractors: A small thing you wear on your wrist to know the day.
A book where you write down important days and events.
A small machine that makes a loud sound to wake you up.

close.definition: The end of something. When something stops.
close.exampleSentence: The close of the show was very good.
close.contextPassage: The close of something is when it finishes. We use this word for the end of a day or an event.
distractors: The part of something that comes at the very start.
The middle part of a story or an event.
A break in the middle when you stop and then start again.

clothes.definition: Things you wear on your body, like a shirt or pants.
clothes.exampleSentence: She puts on clean clothes every day.
clothes.contextPassage: You wear clothes to stay warm or look nice. A shop sells many clothes like shirts and dresses.
distractors: A soft thing you sleep on at night.
A bag you carry things in.
A cover you put on a bed.

club.definition: A group of people who meet to do the same thing together.
club.exampleSentence: I am in a football club at school.
club.contextPassage: People in a club meet on the same day every week. They do fun things together, like play sports.
distractors: A place where you buy food and drinks.
A building where you sleep when you travel.
A big room where you study and read books.

coat.definition: A big warm piece of clothing you wear over your other clothes.
coat.exampleSentence: He wears a coat when it is cold outside.
coat.contextPassage: You put on a coat before you go outside in winter. A coat has two long sleeves and is big.
distractors: Something you put on your head to stay warm.
Warm things you wear on your feet and legs.
A soft thing you wrap around your neck.

coffee.definition: A hot drink that is brown and has a strong taste.
coffee.exampleSentence: My dad drinks coffee every morning.
coffee.contextPassage: Many people drink coffee in the morning to wake up. You make it with hot water and coffee powder.
distractors: A cold sweet drink made from fruit.
A hot drink made with milk and tea leaves.
A drink made with cold water and sugar.

cold.definition: Something is cold when it has a very low temperature. It is not warm or hot.
cold.exampleSentence: The water in the glass is very cold.
cold.contextPassage: Ice is cold. You feel cold when you go outside in winter.
distractors: Having a very high temperature, like fire or the sun.
Having a nice temperature, not too hot and not too cold.
Having a strong taste, like a lemon or a lime.

college.definition: A school where big students go to learn after high school.
college.exampleSentence: My sister goes to college in the city.
college.contextPassage: Students go to college to learn more about one big topic. College students are usually older than eighteen.
distractors: A small school for very young children to play and learn.
A place where doctors work and help sick people.
A building where people go to read and borrow books.

colour.definition: The quality that makes things look red, blue, green, or yellow.
colour.contextPassage: You can see many colours in a rainbow. Red, yellow, and green are all colours.
distractors: The way something feels when you touch it, like soft or hard.
How big or small something is.
The sound that something makes when you hit it.

come.definition: To move to a place where someone or something is.
come.exampleSentence: Please come here and sit with me.
come.contextPassage: You come to a place when you move towards it. A friend can come to your house to visit you.
distractors: To move away from a place and not come back.
To stay in one place and not move at all.
To look for something you cannot find.

computer.definition: A machine that you use to write, play, and find information.
computer.exampleSentence: She uses a computer at school every day.
computer.contextPassage: A computer has a screen and a keyboard. You can use a computer to watch videos or write words.
distractors: A small machine you use to talk to people far away.
A machine you use to print words on paper.
A machine that plays music very loud in a room.

concert.definition: A show where people play music for other people to listen to.
concert.exampleSentence: We go to a concert to hear live music.
concert.contextPassage: At a concert, a singer or band plays music on a stage. Many people come to watch and listen together.
distractors: A show where people dance and tell a story with no words.
A big game where two teams play sport together.
A show where people act and say words on a stage.

cook.definition: A person whose job is to make food for other people.
cook.exampleSentence: The cook makes soup for everyone at lunch.
cook.contextPassage: A cook works in a kitchen to make meals. A cook in a big restaurant makes food for many people.
distractors: A person who brings food to your table in a restaurant.
A person who grows vegetables and fruit on a farm.
A person who sells food and drinks in a shop.

cooking.definition: To make food hot and ready to eat, using fire or a stove.
cooking.exampleSentence: My mum is cooking dinner in the kitchen.
cooking.contextPassage: You need a pan or pot for cooking on a stove. Cooking food makes it warm, soft, and safe to eat.
distractors: To put food in a cold place to keep it fresh.
To cut food into small pieces with a knife.
To put food on a plate and give it to someone.

cool.definition: A feeling that is not hot and not cold. It is a little cold but nice.
cool.exampleSentence: I sit in the cool under the tree.
cool.contextPassage: On a hot day, the cool feels good. You can find cool near water or in the shade.
distractors: A feeling that is very, very cold. It can hurt your skin.
A feeling that is very hot. You can get it from the sun or fire.
A feeling that is warm and soft. It feels like a sunny day.

correct.definition: Right and with no mistakes. It is true and good.
correct.contextPassage: A correct answer is not wrong. Your teacher says 'correct' when you do well.
distractors: Not right and with many mistakes. It is not true.
Very good and very fast. You do it better than other people.
New and done for the first time. Nobody did it before.

cost.definition: To need money to buy something. You pay for it.
cost.contextPassage: When something costs a lot, you need much money. You can ask 'How much does it cost?'
distractors: To give something to another person. They can keep it.
To find something on the floor. You pick it up.
To make something new. You use your hands to do it.

could.definition: To be able to do something. You have the skill or power to do it.
could.exampleSentence: She could swim very well at age six.
could.contextPassage: We use 'could' to talk about things in the past. You say 'I could run fast' about a time before now.
distractors: To want to do something very much. You feel it in your heart.
To need to do something. You have no choice.
To try to do something but not finish it. You stop before the end.

country.definition: A big area of land with its own people and leaders. France and Japan are countries.
country.exampleSentence: I want to visit a new country.
country.contextPassage: Every country has a name and a flag. People in a country often speak the same language.
distractors: A very big city with many tall buildings and many people.
A small area inside a city. It has streets and houses.
A piece of land with water all around it. You need a boat to go there.

course.definition: Things that happen one after another. Events go in order from start to end.
course.exampleSentence: A lot happened in the course of the day.
course.contextPassage: Events in a course go in order. First one thing happens, then another thing happens.
distractors: A fast way to go from one place to another. You use a car or train.
One single thing that happens. It is just one moment.
A big change that makes everything different. Nothing is the same after.

cousin.definition: The child of your aunt or uncle. This person is family but not your sister or brother.
cousin.exampleSentence: My cousin comes to my house today.
cousin.contextPassage: Your cousin is the son or daughter of your mother's or father's sister or brother. You meet your cousin at family parties.
distractors: The sister or brother of your mother or father. This person is older family.
A person your mother or father is married to. They live in your home.
The child of your mother and father. You grow up together in the same home.

cow.definition: A big female farm animal. It gives us milk to drink.
cow.exampleSentence: The cow eats grass in the field.
cow.contextPassage: A cow lives on a farm. Farmers get milk from cows every day.
distractors: A big farm animal with a very long neck. It eats leaves from tall trees.
A small farm animal. It gives us eggs to eat.
A big pink farm animal. It likes to play in the mud.

cream.definition: The thick white part of milk. It floats to the top of the milk.
cream.exampleSentence: I put cream in my coffee.
cream.contextPassage: Cream comes from milk. You can put cream on fruit or in hot drinks.
distractors: A sweet white food made from milk and sugar. It is very cold.
A thin drink that is white. It comes from animals.
A yellow food you put on bread. It is soft and made from milk.

cup.definition: A small thing you drink from. It has a handle and you hold it in your hand.
cup.exampleSentence: I drink hot tea from my cup.
cup.contextPassage: You use a cup for hot drinks like tea or coffee. A cup is smaller than a bowl.
distractors: A big round thing you eat food from. It is flat and wide.
A tall thing you drink water from. It has no handle and you can see through it.
A small soft bag you put food in. You take it to school or work.

customer.definition: A person who buys something from a shop. They pay money for things.
customer.exampleSentence: The customer buys a big cake.
customer.contextPassage: A customer goes to a shop and pays for things. Shops need customers to make money.
distractors: A person who works in a shop and helps people. They do not buy things.
A person who makes food or things to sell. They work in a big room.
A person who brings things to your home. They come to your door.

cut.definition: When you use something sharp to make two pieces. A knife makes a cut.
cut.exampleSentence: The cut on my hand is small.
cut.contextPassage: You make a cut with a knife or scissors. A cut can hurt if it is on your skin.
distractors: A mark you make on paper with a pen. It is a line or a dot.
A small dark spot on your skin. It is not from a knife.
A big hole in the ground. It is made by water or people digging.

evening.definition: The part of the day when it gets dark and the sun goes down.
evening.exampleSentence: We eat dinner in the evening.
evening.contextPassage: The evening comes after the afternoon. Many people rest or watch TV in the evening.
distractors: The part of the day when the sun comes up and it gets light.
The part of the day in the middle, when the sun is very high.
The time when you sleep and close your eyes.

every.definition: All people or things in a group, not one missing.
every.exampleSentence: I brush my teeth every day.
every.contextPassage: You use every to talk about all days, all people, or all things. Every comes before a word like day, week, or person.
distractors: Only one person or thing in a group.
A few people or things, but not all.
No people or things at all.

everybody.definition: All people, not one person missing.
everybody.exampleSentence: Everybody in the class is happy today.
everybody.contextPassage: Everybody means all the people in a place or group. You use it like a name for all people together.
distractors: Only one person in a room or group.
A few people but not all of them.
No people at all in a place.

everyone.definition: Every single person, all people together.
everyone.exampleSentence: Everyone likes ice cream at the party.
everyone.contextPassage: Everyone means all the people you are talking about. It is the same as everybody.
distractors: Only one special person in a group.
Some people but not all of them.
A person you do not know.

everything.definition: All the things, not one thing missing.
everything.exampleSentence: I put everything in my bag.
everything.contextPassage: Everything means all the things in a place or group. You can use it when you do not want to name each thing.
distractors: Only one small thing in a place.
A few things but not all of them.
No things at all in a place.

exam.definition: A big test at school to see what you know.
exam.exampleSentence: I have a big exam at school today.
exam.contextPassage: You study hard before an exam. The teacher reads your answers and gives you a score.
distractors: A book you read to learn new things at school.
A person who helps you learn new words and skills.
A place where you go to study with other students.

example.definition: One thing that shows what all things like it are.
example.exampleSentence: A dog is an example of an animal.
example.contextPassage: A teacher uses an example to help you understand. You can say for example before you show one thing from a group.
distractors: A rule that tells you what you must do or not do.
A question you must answer in a test at school.
A list of all the things in a group.

excited.definition: Feeling very happy and full of energy about something.
excited.exampleSentence: I am excited about my birthday party.
excited.contextPassage: You feel excited before something fun or new. Your heart goes fast and you feel very happy.
distractors: Feeling very tired and wanting to sleep.
Feeling sad because something is not good.
Feeling scared because something is big or new.

exciting.definition: Something that makes you feel very happy and full of energy.
exciting.exampleSentence: The football game is very exciting to watch.
exciting.contextPassage: You say something is exciting when it makes you feel great. A fun trip or a good game can be exciting.
distractors: Something that makes you feel very tired and sleepy.
Something that makes you feel sad and not happy.
Something that is very easy and not new or fun.

exercise.definition: When you move your body to be strong and healthy.
exercise.exampleSentence: I do exercise every morning in the park.
exercise.contextPassage: Running and swimming are good exercise for your body. Exercise helps you feel good and stay healthy.
distractors: When you sit and read a book to learn new things.
When you eat good food to stay healthy and strong.
When you sleep to help your body rest and get better.

expensive.definition: Something that costs a lot of money to buy.
expensive.exampleSentence: This new phone is very expensive.
expensive.contextPassage: You use expensive to talk about things that are not cheap. You need a lot of money to buy an expensive thing.
distractors: Something that costs very little money and is easy to buy.
Something that is very big and heavy to carry.
Something that is very old and not new or good.

eye.definition: The part of your face that you use to see things.
eye.contextPassage: You use your eyes to look at things. When you sleep, you close your eyes.
distractors: The part of your face that you use to smell things.
The part of your face that you use to eat food.
The part of your head that you use to hear sounds.

face.definition: The front part of your head. It has your eyes, nose, and mouth.
face.exampleSentence: She has a happy face today.
face.contextPassage: You can see a person's face when you look at them. Eyes, a nose, and a mouth are on the face.
distractors: The top part of your head where hair grows.
The back part of your head.
The side part of your head near your ear.

fall.definition: When something goes down from a high place to a low place.
fall.exampleSentence: The ball had a big fall from the table.
fall.contextPassage: A fall can happen fast or slow. After a fall, something is on the ground.
distractors: When something goes up high in the air.
When something moves very fast from side to side.
When something stays in one place and does not move.

false.definition: The answer you choose when something is not true.
false.exampleSentence: The answer to that question is false.
false.contextPassage: In a test, you can choose true or false. You choose false when the words are not right.
distractors: The answer you choose when something is right and correct.
A question that has many answers to choose from.
A question that asks you to write a long answer.

family.definition: A group of people who love each other and are related. For example, a mother, father, and children.
family.exampleSentence: My family eats dinner together every day.
family.contextPassage: A family can be big or small. Your mother, father, and brothers or sisters are your family.
distractors: A group of friends who go to the same school.
People who work together in the same place.
A group of people who live in the same street.

famous.definition: Known by many, many people all over the world.
famous.exampleSentence: This singer is very famous around the world.
famous.contextPassage: A famous person is on TV and in many photos. Many people know their name.
distractors: Known only by people in your family.
Known only by people in your school.
Not known by anyone at all.

fantastic.definition: Very, very good. Better than good or great.
fantastic.exampleSentence: This pizza is fantastic, I love it!
fantastic.contextPassage: You say fantastic when something makes you very happy. It is a very strong, good word.
distractors: A little bit good, but not very good.
Very bad and not nice at all.
Not good and not bad, just ok.

far.definition: A long way from here. Not close or near.
far.exampleSentence: The school is far from my house.
far.contextPassage: When something is far, you need a long time to get there. A car or bus can help you go to far places.
distractors: Very close and easy to get to quickly.
Next to you or right here with you.
On the other side of a small room.

farm.definition: A big place where people grow food or keep animals.
farm.exampleSentence: There are many cows on that farm.
farm.contextPassage: A farm has animals like cows and chickens. People also grow fruit and vegetables on a farm.
distractors: A big building where people make cars and machines.
A big shop where you can buy food and clothes.
A big place where children go to learn and play.

farmer.definition: A person who works on a farm. They grow food and look after animals.
farmer.exampleSentence: The farmer feeds the animals every morning.
farmer.contextPassage: A farmer works outside a lot. They grow vegetables and fruit for us to eat.
distractors: A person who sells food in a big shop.
A person who drives a big truck on long roads.
A person who cooks food in a restaurant.

fast.definition: Moving or going quickly. Not slow.
fast.exampleSentence: The fast train goes to the big city.
fast.contextPassage: A fast train does not stop at every station. It gets to the big city very quickly.
distractors: A train that stops at every small station on the way.
A train that only goes at night when it is dark.
A very old train that people look at but do not ride.

fat.definition: A soft part inside animals and food that has a lot of energy in it.
fat.exampleSentence: This meat has a lot of fat on it.
fat.contextPassage: Fat is found in meat and some foods. Our body uses fat to get energy.
distractors: The hard part inside your arm or leg that holds you up.
The red part inside meat that gives it color.
The soft part of fruit that you eat and enjoy.

father.definition: A man who has a child. He is the boy parent in a family.
father.exampleSentence: My father is very tall and kind.
father.contextPassage: A father is a man who has one or more children. You can also call him dad.
distractors: A woman who has a child and is the girl parent.
A man who is the brother of your mother or father.
A man who is older and lives in your street.

favourite.definition: The one thing or person you like most.
favourite.contextPassage: You use this word to talk about what you like best. You can have a favourite food, colour, or song.
distractors: A thing you do not like at all.
A thing you see every day.
A thing that is very big and old.

february.definition: The second month of the year.
february.contextPassage: February comes after January. It is a short month with 28 or 29 days.
distractors: The first month of the year.
The last month of the year.
The month that comes after March.

feel.definition: To know if something is hot, cold, soft, or hard by touching it.
feel.contextPassage: You use this word when your body or your heart tells you something. You can feel happy, sad, or sick.
distractors: To look at something with your eyes.
To eat something with your mouth.
To listen to a sound with your ears.

feeling.definition: Something inside you that tells you if you are happy, sad, or scared.
feeling.exampleSentence: I have a good feeling about today.
feeling.contextPassage: A feeling is not a thing you can touch or see. Happy, sad, and angry are all feelings.
distractors: A thing you can hold in your hand.
A sound you hear in the street.
A food that is very sweet.

festival.definition: A big, fun event where many people come together to celebrate something.
festival.exampleSentence: We go to a music festival every summer.
festival.contextPassage: A festival can have music, food, and games. Many people come and have a good time.
distractors: A place where you buy food every day.
A small room where you can rest and sleep.
A class where children go to learn and study.

few.definition: A small number of things or people, not many.
few.exampleSentence: I have a few apples in my bag.
few.contextPassage: You use few when the number is small. For example, two or three things is a few.
distractors: A very big number of things.
All the things in one place.
Not one thing at all.

fifteen number.definition: The number that is ten and five together, 15.
fifteen number.contextPassage: Fifteen comes after fourteen. You can count to fifteen on your fingers and toes.
distractors: The number that is ten and four together, 14.
The number that is ten and six together, 16.
The number that is ten and three together, 13.

fifth number.definition: The one that comes after the fourth, number five in a line.
fifth number.exampleSentence: She is fifth in the race.
fifth number.contextPassage: You use fifth to say where something is in a line or list. First, second, third, fourth, fifth.
distractors: The one that comes after the third, number four in a line.
The one that comes after the fifth, number six in a line.
The one that is first and at the very start.

fifty number.definition: The number that is five tens together, 50.
fifty number.exampleSentence: There are fifty children in the school.
fifty number.contextPassage: Fifty comes after forty-nine. It is a big number you can count to.
distractors: The number that is four tens together, 40.
The number that is six tens together, 60.
The number that is three tens together, 30.

fill.definition: To put things into something until it is full.
fill.exampleSentence: Please fill my cup with water.
fill.contextPassage: You can fill a bag, a cup, or a box. When it is full, you stop putting things in.
distractors: To take everything out of something.
To break something into small parts.
To look for something you cannot see.

film.definition: A very thin layer that covers the top of something.
film.exampleSentence: There is a film of oil on the water.
film.contextPassage: A film is very thin, like a skin on top of something. You can sometimes see it on water or on food.
distractors: A very loud sound you hear outside.
A thick, heavy piece of hard rock.
A big, deep hole in the ground.

find.definition: Something good or special that you discover or see for the first time.
find.exampleSentence: This old coin is a great find.
find.contextPassage: You use this word when you discover something that is new or special. People who dig in the ground can make a great find.
distractors: Something old and broken that nobody wants.
A place where many people live and work.
A plan you make before you do something.

fine.definition: A strong drink made from grapes. It is like wine but stronger.
fine.exampleSentence: My dad drinks fine after dinner.
fine.contextPassage: Fine is a drink from France. It is a very strong drink for adults.
distractors: A cold drink made from fruit and water. It is sweet and good.
A hot drink made with tea leaves and water. People drink it in the morning.
A drink made from apples. It is sweet and cold.

finish.definition: The end of something. It is when something stops.
finish.exampleSentence: I can see the finish of the race.
finish.contextPassage: The finish is the last part. After the finish, it is all done.
distractors: The start of something. It is when something begins for the first time.
The middle part of something. It is not the start and not the end.
A very fast part of a race. You run very fast here.

fire.definition: A very hot and bright thing. It gives heat and light and can burn things.
fire.exampleSentence: The fire is big and very hot.
fire.contextPassage: Fire can be good. We use it to cook food and stay warm.
distractors: Cold water that falls from the sky. It is wet and soft.
A big light in the sky at night. It is very far away and small.
Very cold white water on the ground. It falls in winter.

first.definition: The one that comes before all others. It is number one in a line.
first.exampleSentence: She is first in the line today.
first.contextPassage: First means you are at the start. You are not second or third.
distractors: The one that comes at the very end. No one comes after this one.
The one that is in the middle. It is not first and not last.
The one that is the same as another. They look the same.

fish.definition: An animal that lives in water. It has fins and can swim.
fish.exampleSentence: I can see a big fish in the water.
fish.contextPassage: A fish lives in rivers and the sea. It uses its fins to swim fast.
distractors: A small animal that lives in the ground. It is brown and soft.
A big animal that lives on land and has four legs. It eats grass.
A small animal with wings. It can fly in the sky.

five.definition: The number 5. It comes after four and before six.
five.exampleSentence: I have five apples in my bag.
five.contextPassage: Five is a number. You can count to five on one hand.
distractors: The number 4. It comes after three and before five.
The number 6. It comes after five and before seven.
The number 10. It is two times five.

flat.definition: A piece of land that is not high and not low. It is very even and smooth.
flat.exampleSentence: We play football on the flat.
flat.contextPassage: A flat is a place where the ground is level. It is easy to walk on a flat.
distractors: A very high piece of land. You can see far from the top.
A place where there is a lot of water. Animals live there.
A place with a lot of trees and plants. It is big and green.

flight.definition: When something or someone moves through the air. It is like how a bird moves.
flight.exampleSentence: The bird's flight is fast and beautiful.
flight.contextPassage: Flight is moving up in the air. Birds and planes do this.
distractors: When something moves fast on the ground. It goes from one place to another.
When something moves slowly in the water. It goes up and down.
When something stops and does not move at all.

floor.definition: The flat part inside a room that you walk on. It is at the bottom of a room.
floor.exampleSentence: My bag is on the floor.
floor.contextPassage: You walk on the floor every day. The floor can be made of wood or stone.
distractors: The top part of a room. It is up above your head.
The part of a room on the side. You can put pictures on it.
The part outside a house. You walk on it to go inside.

flower.definition: A pretty part of a plant. It has a nice color and sometimes smells good.
flower.exampleSentence: I give her a red flower today.
flower.contextPassage: A flower grows on a plant. Many flowers are red, yellow, or white.
distractors: The green part of a plant. It is flat and grows on a branch.
The part of a plant under the ground. It holds the plant in place.
The long green part of a plant. It holds the flower up.

fly.definition: A very small insect with two wings. It can fly and it likes food.
fly.exampleSentence: There is a fly on my food.
fly.contextPassage: A fly has two small wings. It is a very small insect and moves very fast.
distractors: A small insect that makes honey. It has yellow and black colors.
A small insect that gives light at night. You can see it in the dark.
A small insect with big wings. It is many colors and very pretty.
